food_subgroup: Sandwich meal set — beef brisket sub with cookie side

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for a BBQ beef brisket submarine sandwich meal set with cookie. FOODON_00002473 (beef sandwich) is the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (beef sandwich) used as closest parent. No specific code exists for a BBQ brisket sub meal set with cookie in FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for a commercial BBQ beef brisket sandwich meal set. SNOMED CT does not provide granular fast-food meal set codes.

serving.description: 1 meal set (6-inch or footlong sub with cookie)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Subway meal set consisting of a 6-inch or footlong smoky BBQ beef brisket submarine sandwich with vegetables and one cookie, totalling approximately 380 g.

nutrient_source.title: Subway Smoky BBQ Beef Brisket Sub — USDA Branded Food and Subway Nutritional Data adapted with USDA SR Legacy beef brisket and cookie components
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Branded Foods and SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values estimated by component-level adaptation using USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entries for Subway beef brisket sub (FDC ID 2097663 and related entries) and USDA SR Legacy data for beef brisket (FDC ID 174032) and chocolate chip cookie. Per-100g values derived from a representative 380g meal set (6-inch sub ~220g + cookie ~45g + vegetables ~115g). Values represent a weighted composite. Confidence is medium due to menu variability and bread-option dependency noted in source record.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mixed beef and bread sandwich — analogous estimate based on white or wholegrain submarine roll GI data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a Subway BBQ beef brisket meal set. GI estimated at 53 (low) based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for white submarine-style bread rolls (GI ~55) and mixed beef sandwiches (GI ~46–55 range). BBQ sauce adds moderate sugar load. Cookie component raises overall GL. Composite GI of 53 is a defensible analogous estimate. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (53 × 118.6) / 100 ≈ 37.0 for a 380g meal set containing approximately 118.6g carbohydrate.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg per 100g; ~1976 mg per meal set)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this meal set due to its very high sodium content (~1976 mg per serving), which approaches or exceeds the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg for hypertensive patients; choosing lower-sodium bread options and omitting BBQ sauce can partially reduce sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~37 per meal set) driven by refined bread roll and BBQ sauce sugars
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious with this meal set due to its moderate-to-high glycemic load of approximately 37 per serving; selecting wholegrain bread, omitting the cookie, and reducing BBQ sauce can meaningfully lower the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~2.9g per 100g), trans fat (~0.1g per 100g), and high sodium from processed beef and BBQ sauce
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this meal set in moderation given its saturated fat, trace trans fat, and high sodium content; opting for leaner protein alternatives, wholegrain bread, and reduced-sodium condiments is advisable to support cardiovascular health.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Smoky BBQ Beef Brisket with Cookie (Subway Meal) is a commercial fast-food meal set sold at Subway outlets in Singapore, including Subway Funan and the Funan Mix & Match outlet. The meal consists of a submarine sandwich filled with slow-smoked, BBQ-sauced beef brisket layered with fresh vegetables (lettuce, tomato, onion) on a choice of bread roll (white, wholegrain, or Italian herbs and cheese, subject to availability), accompanied by one Subway cookie (typically chocolate chip or oatmeal raisin). The beef brisket is characterised by a smoky, sweet-savoury flavour profile from the BBQ sauce, with a tender, pull-apart texture. The cookie adds a sweet, buttery finish to the meal. Nutritionally, the meal set is energy-dense (~942 kcal per 380g serving), high in sodium (~1976 mg per serving), and provides moderate protein (~48.6g per serving) and significant carbohydrate (~118.6g per serving) from the bread roll, BBQ sauce, and cookie. The meal is clinically relevant for individuals managing hypertension (very high sodium), Type 2 Diabetes (moderate-to-high glycemic load), and cardiovascular disease (saturated fat and sodium). It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those on gluten-free diets. The price point is SGD 15.60 as listed on field survey data.

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN entry exists for this specific Subway meal set. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods (Subway beef brisket sub entries) and USDA SR Legacy component data. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using comparable sandwich and bread roll entries. Confidence is medium due to menu variability, bread-option dependency, and the composite nature of the meal set.
notes: 1. Bread options are subject to availability as noted in the source record (field survey data). Nutrient values may vary depending on bread choice (white vs wholegrain vs Italian herbs). 2. The meal set price is SGD 15.60 as listed on field survey data (Subway Funan and Funan Mix & Match). 3. Serving size of 380g is an estimate for a 6-inch sub (~220g) plus one cookie (~45g) plus vegetables (~115g); a footlong version would approximately double the sub component. 4. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entries for this product. 5. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entries for this product. 6. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entries for this product. 7. Halal status is not confirmed for this specific Subway outlet; Subway Singapore halal certification varies by outlet. 8. The food_group has been corrected from the input value of 'Desserts' to 'Mixed' as this is a sandwich meal set, not a dessert. 9. The cuisine has been retained as Western/Sandwich consistent with the input cuisine tag. 10. GI set to 53 (low) as an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database sandwich entries; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific product.

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected food_group from 'Desserts' (input error) to 'Mixed' appropriate for a sandwich meal set.
Constructed canonical food_name replacing promotional field survey menu title.
Added full multilingual local_names for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi per Singaporean dish requirements.
Built comprehensive aliases and aliases_by_language with full consistency verification.
Derived per_100g nutrient values via component-level adaptation from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods and SR Legacy.
Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) with explicit source-non-reporting justification in notes.
Estimated GI at 53 (low) from University of Sydney GI Database analogous sandwich entries with full documentation.
Calculated GL per serving as 37.0 based on 380g serving size and carbohydrate content.
Assigned FoodOn and FoodEx2 closest_parent codes with gap notes; SNOMED CT set to none with gap note.
Wrote clinical health context for Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and Cardiovascular Disease with specific guidance.
Wrote LLM natural_language_description exceeding 200 characters with full clinical and nutritional context.
